page: Amazon Web Services r8idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, which is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

This instance includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local storage directly attached to the Nitro hypervisor. Additionally, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, optimized for both network throughput and Amazon Elastic Block Store connectivity.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8idn.16xlarge consolidates massive memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high network throughput into a single virtualized resource pool, reducing the need for multi-instance clustering for large datasets. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data analytics, enterprise applications, and high-throughput transactional workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 512.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This combination of high-capacity memory, fast local storage, and high-speed networking makes it highly efficient for resource-dense workloads. It is designed for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and enterprise applications requiring high-throughput, low-latency data access.
